name: Hunza Valley
description: A vibrant mountainous valley known for its breathtaking views of Rakaposhi, ancient forts like Baltit and Altit, and the warm hospitality of its locals.

name: Fairy Meadows
description: A lush green alpine meadow that offers a stunning, up-close view of the mighty Nanga Parbat, the killer mountain, surrounded by pine forests.

name: Attabad Lake
description: Famous for its stunning turquoise-blue waters, this breathtaking lake was formed by a massive landslide in 2010 and sits amid towering mountains.

name: Badshahi Mosque
description: A masterpiece of Mughal architecture commissioned by Emperor Aurangzeb, featuring iconic red sandstone and white marble domes that have stood since 1673.

name: Faisal Mosque
description: An iconic desert tent-structured mosque situated at the foot of Margalla Hills, designed by Turkish architect Vedat Dalokay — one of the largest mosques in the world.

name: Deosai National Park
description: Known as the 'Land of Giants', it is one of the highest plateaus in the world, famous for its wildflowers in summer and the rare Himalayan brown bear.

name: Saif-ul-Malook Lake
description: A legendary alpine lake associated with the romantic fairy tale of Prince Saif-ul-Malook, surrounded by snow-capped peaks at an altitude of 3,224 metres.

name: Mohenjo-daro
description: An archaeological site dating back to 2500 BCE, representing one of the world's earliest major settlements and an UNESCO World Heritage Site of the Indus Valley Civilization.

name: Malam Jabba
description: A premier ski resort in the Hindu Kush mountain range, popular for winter sports, scenic chairlifts, and panoramic views of the lush Swat Valley.

name: Clifton Beach
description: A bustling Arabian Sea beach in Karachi, famous for its silver sand, camel rides, and vibrant local street food culture, frequented by thousands daily.

name: Lahore Fort
description: A grand UNESCO World Heritage fortress complex built by Mughal emperors, featuring stunning palaces, gardens, and the famous Sheesh Mahal (Palace of Mirrors).

name: Minar-e-Pakistan
description: A majestic national monument marking the site where the Pakistan Resolution was passed in 1940, standing tall as a symbol of the nation's independence.

name: Neelum Valley
description: A stunning river valley in Azad Kashmir, renowned for its crystal-clear Neelum River, dense forests, and picturesque villages wrapped in lush green mountains.

name: Kalam Valley
description: A breathtaking valley in upper Swat, known for its roaring rivers, lush meadows, dense pine forests, and the beautiful Mahodand Lake nestled above it.

name: Pakistan Monument
description: A stunning four-petal flower-shaped monument representing the four provinces of Pakistan, offering panoramic views of Islamabad from Shakarparian Hill.

name: Margalla Hills
description: A range of foothill hills forming a national park right at the edge of Islamabad, perfect for hiking, birdwatching, and panoramic views of the capital.

name: Skardu Valley
description: The gateway to the Karakoram mountain range and base camp for K2, Skardu offers dramatic desert landscapes, ancient forts, and stunning cold-water lakes.

name: Nathia Gali
description: A charming hill station in the Galyat region, nestled in the pine-covered mountains, known for its pleasant climate, scenic trails, and colonial-era churches.

name: Taxila
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring one of the greatest ancient Buddhist centres of learning, with ruins dating back to the 5th century BCE.

name: Shandur Pass
description: Known as the 'Roof of the World', Shandur Pass at 3,700m hosts the annual Shandur Polo Festival, considered the world's highest polo ground.

name: Makli Necropolis
description: One of the largest funerary sites in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site, containing the tombs of rulers, saints and philosophers from the 14th to 18th centuries.

name: Naltar Valley
description: A hidden gem valley famous for its multi-coloured lakes, dense pine forests, and some of Pakistan's best skiing slopes, accessible via a thrilling jeep track.

name: Ranikot Fort
description: Considered the world's largest fort, nicknamed the 'Great Wall of Sindh', with walls stretching over 32 km across the rugged Sindh mountains.

name: Tharparkar Desert
description: Pakistan's only fertile desert with a rich cultural heritage, dotted with ancient Hindu temples, unique Rajputana architecture, and vibrant local festivals.

name: Khewra Salt Mine
description: The world's second-largest salt mine and Pakistan's largest, with a history dating back to 1200 BC, featuring stunning illuminated salt chambers and a salt mosque inside.

name: Gwadar
description: A deep-sea port city on the Arabian Sea coast, featuring pristine beaches, the iconic Hammerhead Rock, and the CPEC hub with stunning ocean views.

name: Quetta
description: The 'Fruit Garden of Pakistan', Quetta is surrounded by majestic mountains and is famous for its dry fruits, Hanna Lake, and the scenic Bolan Pass.

name: Chiltan National Park
description: A national park surrounding the Chiltan mountain near Quetta, home to the rare Chiltan wild goat (Markhor), diverse flora, and spectacular mountain views.

name: Gorakh Hill Station
description: The only hill station in Sindh, perched at 1,734 metres, Gorakh offers a cool escape from the scorching plains with stunning views of the surrounding valleys.

name: Murree
description: Pakistan's most popular hill station with a rich British colonial legacy, pine-covered slopes, the lively Mall Road, and a gateway to the beautiful Galyat range.
